query: STF plenary calendar June 2026 schedule sessions Marco Civil proclamation
answer:
## STF Plenary Calendar — June 2026: Sessions & Marco Civil da Internet

### 1. Plenary Sessions Scheduled (June 15–30, 2026)

**Virtual Plenary (Plenário Virtual):**
- A Virtual Plenary session is **currently in progress**, having started on **Friday, June 12, 2026 at 11:00 AM** and running through **Thursday, June 19, 2026 at 23:59**.

**Physical (Presencial) Plenary Sessions:**
- The STF physical plenary typically convenes on **Wednesdays and Thursdays** during regular session periods.
- The most recent physical sessions were held on **June 10 (Tuesday)** and **June 11 (Wednesday), 2026**.
- The **next confirmed physical plenary session is Wednesday, June 17, 2026** — explicitly confirmed by STF news coverage stating "Julgamento deverá ser retomado na próxima quarta (17)."
- Following the standard pattern, additional sessions are expected on **June 18 (Thu), June 24 (Wed), and June 25 (Thu)**, though only June 17 was explicitly confirmed in available sources.

> **Note:** The official STF calendar pages at `portal.stf.jus.br` returned 403 errors, so the above dates are derived from STF news articles.

---

### 2. Marco Civil da Internet — Actively on the Agenda

The Marco Civil da Internet case is **actively being adjudicated** and is **confirmed on the plenary agenda** for the next session.

**Current Phase:** The Court is in the phase of analyzing **embargos de declaração** (clarification motions/appeals) against the thesis of general repercussion fixed in the original Marco Civil judgment. This is the "aperfeiçoamento da tese" (refinement of the thesis) stage.

**Recent Timeline:**
| Date | Event |
|------|-------|
| **June 10, 2026** | STF began analysis of appeals against the Marco Civil thesis |
| **June 11, 2026** | STF advanced discussion on adjustments to the thesis; session adjourned with resumption scheduled for June 17 |
| **June 13, 2026** | STF podcast ("Supremo na Semana" #193) highlighted the Marco Civil thesis refinement as the week's top story |
| **June 17, 2026** | **Next scheduled session** — Marco Civil judgment expected to resume |

**Prior Ruling:** The STF has already concluded that **Article 19 of the Marco Civil da Internet is partially unconstitutional**.

**Key Consensus Already Reached:**
- Platforms will have a **60-day deadline** to implement structural changes, including:
  - **Duty of care** (dever de cuidado)
  - **Self-regulation** (autorregulação)
  - **Dedicated complaint channels** for users to file reports

---

### 3. Key Takeaways

- The Marco Civil proclamation is **not yet finalized** — the Court is still refining the thesis through the embargos de declaração process.
- The **next opportunity for the proclamation** (formal conclusion) is **Wednesday, June 17, 2026**, when the physical plenary resumes.
- There is already significant consensus among the ministers on the 60-day implementation framework, suggesting the refinement phase may conclude soon.

---
